brief_justification: Correctly identifies 'Higher' with valid reasoning that the third description is more general/mathematical than the engineer's; constructs a full three-level abstraction ladder and explains the specificity-vs-generality tradeoff at multiple levels; proceeds linearly with no named biases, assumption checks, or course-corrections; constructs an abstraction ladder that implicitly cross-checks placement but applies no explicit counterfactual or boundary-condition test; presents conclusion confidently without acknowledging uncertainty or what evidence could flip the answer; never considers whether the third description might be at the same level or lower—goes straight to 'Higher'; every conclusion is explicitly derived with traceable steps (class-of-failure argument, one-instance subsumption, abstraction ladder), fully reproducible by a third party.
